title: SQL injection in readSchema via string interpolation
description: The readSchema function constructs a SQL query using string interpolation with the 'table' parameter: "SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='\(table)' LIMIT 1;". While the 'table' parameter currently comes from hardcoded strings within the same class (e.g., 'users', 'groups', 'user_privileges', 'group_privileges'), this is a dangerous pattern. If any future code path passes user-controlled data to readSchema, it would enable SQL injection. The function does not use parameterized queries.
vulnerable_code:
private func readSchema(db: OpaquePointer, table: String) -> String? {
    let query = "SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='\(table)' LIMIT 1;"
    var statement: OpaquePointer?
    guard sqlitePrepare(db: db, query: query, statement: &statement) == SQLITE_OK ...
recommendation: Use parameterized queries with sqlite3_bind_text instead of string interpolation. Also, the migratePrivilegesTableIfNeeded function at line 258 uses string interpolation for table names in DDL statements (which cannot use bind parameters), so at minimum add a whitelist validation: guard ["users", "groups", "user_privileges", "group_privileges"].contains(table).
